Dear Email Player,
I've written about the late comedian Patrice O'Neal in the hallowed pages of the
Email Players inner sanctum before. He was mostly known for doing bits about gender
relations. He was called all kinds of names, of course. But what his haters didn't realize
(mostly feminists and male "white knights" who put chicks on a pedestal) is he wasn'tjust
some angry guy ranting. He had a well thought-out philosophy he had gained from years
of being a 300+ pound guy who chicks would avoid. He did all the poem writing and
supplicating and things guys do that get them blown off for the bad boys. And thus, his
philosophy was very distinct based on reality and observation and field testing. I am not
sq.rng I approved of his lifestyle (I don't at all). But his philosophies on gender relations
are all right on the money. For example, he would say things líke, "If a wuman lcnows;tou
uill righteousfu leaae, she utill righteously stry." (Same can be said for a lot of clients and
customers, too... hint, hint...) And "Women haae hpo things in their head. Thq haae the thing that
m.akes them go 'I LOVEyu. Tou're a MAI{. Tou're bi4gu than me, shonger than me, I can't nen opm
tltis pickk jar withoutltou.' And then it's tlu other side of their brain that goes '%ufaggot ax bitch, wtgt
are)ou loaing thi: motherfucku so much?'(Same can be said of certain clients and customers,
too, especially when after you collect royalties for a while... hint, hint...) And "One thing
tounen needfrom2ou is emotions. [...]. If )ou ftolrnb drflrtt themJ, her emotions haue to eat on
thunsehtes. I¿t her emotions eat her soul up, then slu'll think 0f another option because she's not walking

prepared and even eager for the solution I sell when it's presented. This is the heart
a^nd soul of what I call 55the philosophy" ernail. And it's another example of my
"soft teaching" strategy. When you do this you aren't revealing anything in the product.
You are just talking to your list about what's important to them and giving adüie (but
nothing in your product). You may or may not have the same results (I am the only one I
know who consciously writes emails like this, other than my friendJim Yaghi, as we have
talked at length about this). But philosophy emails sometimes do a betterjob selling than
more aggressive emails that are obüously going for the sale (not that there is anything
wrong with those, see later in this issue). But besides the sales aspect there is another very
cool benefit of doing this: You get to pontificate! Like the great comic book creator
Stan Lee would do with his Silver Surfer character. Or what George Lucas got to do with
Yoda. You get to espouse your thoughts and philosophies on the problem your product or
service solves. This is the single best way I know to (as the world's "most feared"
negotiatorJim Camps teaches) "get in the other person's world" -- where it is impossible
to bore or lose their interest. After all, it's all about THEM -- their problems, deslres, fears
and insecurities. There is no hard teaching. There is no moving free lines. There is just
getting in sync with your customers in a fun, entertaining and thought provoking way.
And at the end of the day, the best customers will almost always more readily respond to
emails that do that than the ones that talk about how today is the last chanc. to g.t in on
your offer.

THE EITEIL PLAYERS NSWSLETTER
truly great emailers often fall into L of 2 "camps":
1". A great email coPlnrvriter
2. A great email marketer
Rarely is someone equally found in both'
A few fall into extremes.
But, most are stightly tilted one way or the other'
Anlnüay, here are some "te1ls" about each:
A great email copyrriter wilt spend hours crafting the perfect
subject line -- each word carefutty chosen using a thesaurus, a
swipe file and a pile of magazines, printed out emails and sales
Ietter headlines. He does this because in his heart (often the
result of being a trained coplnrriter) he thinks the headline
(subject line) is the most important piece of "real estate" of the
entire email.
And, thus the most important part of making the sale'
on the other hand...
A great emait marketer knows money is attracted to speed.
He bats his subject lines out FAST.
(Sometimes it's little more than a brain fart.)
And, he doesn,t \dorry about it either hTay -- counting on the bond
he has with his audience to get a reading more than a subject line
wiIl.
A great email coplnrriter obsesses over metrics.
Like open rates or clickthru rates.
And also which link in the email was cticked most or if the image
made any difference in clicks, and so on.
¡te will be very conversant about email metrics, too'
To him, it's like a video gane:
4-

-- 4 of 16 --

I
THB EIteIT PLAYERS NEwSLETTER
He just rrants to increase his "score" (i.e. his opens and clicks)
each time, and that's how he mainly keeps score.
Not so the great email marketer.
The only "metric" he cares about is did the cash register ring?
If not, the email was a dud.
If it did, it was successful.
Ile would rather have a 1t open rate and get 10 sales than have 1008
opens and zero sales. And if he hits on an especially powerful
"nerve" he will write several more emails right after -- maybe even
for an entire week, milking as much money out of that
theme/idea/problem/hot button as possible -- judging it's time to
talk about something else not by clicks or opens, but by when sales
dwindle down.
A great coplnrriter mails less frequently.
Maybe, at most, a few times per week.
Reason why is, he opts for quality over quantity, takes great pride
in his email writing, and refuses to send anything but the best
crafted story or pitch he possibly can.
He also hates opt outs, too.
And, wiII sometimes even take them personally.
Especially if it's accompanied by a complaint.
Conversely, a great email marketer mails DAILY.
Sometimes even 2-3 TIMES per day, 7 days per week (especially if he
serrs in a rabid market of people who are anxious to sorve whatever
problem his product solves.) In his way of thinking, he,d rather
play to win 1i.e. make more sales) than play not to lose (not anger
his list and get the least amount of opt outs).
Frankly, he celebrates opt outs.
Thinks it's funny when people complain.
And, in some hrays, measures his email's success (besides the sales)

based on the emotional reaction he gets from people who hate him.
A great email copywriter has Iinking down to a science'
He kno\¡Is EXACTLY how many links to put in an email '
EXACTLY where to Put them.
And EXACTLY how many clicks he can expect'
since he obsesses over clickthrus, he knows where all the best
places to put links are for maximum clicks (i.e. above the fold, in
itre ps, etc) and spends a lot of time crafting his emails around
this ',inte},' he,s gained from studying aII his metrics.
A great email marketer often uses just one link'
Usually, at the end.
And, above his signature.
(He puts as little thought as possible into it' )
He is more concerned with having his email seem natural -- Iike a
conversation -- than he is on inserting links into an email based
on where the most clicks occur. Again, he is aII about speed... and
knows the less time he puts into one email, the more he has to put
into others -- so he can have multiple different emails/projects
working hard for him instead of just one or t$lo'
An]¡\^ray, so there You have it.
Differences between great email coplnrriters & great email marketers'
Both have their strengths and weaknesses.'
And, certain personalities naturalty gravitate towards one or the
other. For example, I definitely faII on the side of the marketer
vs the coplnrriter (and have purposely unlearned many habits I
Iearned over the years of writing coPy to make more sales with
emait). I am aII about speed, and getting it done in L5 minutes or
so, then moving on to other things.
Life is too short to be chained to a desk all day'
But, I could use a bit more of the email coplnuriter side.

Moving on:
And then it segued into my product. Again, this is a VERY easy email to adapt for
your wicked ways. Al1you have to do is look at two ways of doing whatever you teach --
the way you do it and the way you don't do it but that still works (there is rarely a one-
size-fits-all solution to everything).
So for example:
If you sell weight loss you can do an email about the difference between your diet
and the Paleo Diet. Or between your diet and Tim Ferris's 4-Hour Body diet. Or between
your way of exercising and doing cardio, etc. You spell out all the pros and cons of both,
with yours (being the superior way, of course)being the obüous choice.
Give this kind of email a shot this month and let me know what happens.

And then it continues to pitch the product and what I CAl\ help with. The point

out like afart in study hall (but in a good way). I recommend doing these as often as
possible -- especially if you are in an overheated market with lots of competition and big
hyp.y claims. Frankly, it's the best way to stick out from all your competitors in just about
any market as the vast majority of emailers simply don't have the balls to do it.

tight" argument. The downside to this kind of writing is it often takes a lot more time (it

So-called "eating disorders" are not only almost completely harmless,
but they are materially beneficial to millions of men and women who
would otherwise be obese and diabetic. And they are both materially and
aesthetically beneficial to the hundreds of millions of men and \iromen
who would otherwise have to look at them, pay taxes for their medicat
care, and bury them.
It is important to understand that anorexia barely kills anyone.
Virtually no one who has it is at any risk of dying from it, as per the
US National Library of Medicine National fnstitutes of Health, which
cited a study entitled "Death from anorexia nervosa: age span and sex
differences".
The purpose of this study was to assess characteristics of individuals
who died from anorexia nervosa by assessing the frequency with which
anorexia nervosa is listed as a causal factor related to the death of
individuars in the usA. Data from over L0 mirlion death records (arr
National Center for Health Statistic registered deaths in the USA for
L986-90) were examined for mention of anorexia nervosa as a primary or
contributing cause of death. orll-y 724 were found, which equars an
average of 145 annuar deaths, and a rate of 6.73 per 100,000 deaths.
The age and sex distribution suggests t\^ro fatal forms of anorexia
nervosa, an early-onset form comprising 89t women and a later form
comprising 24t men. The findings suggest that the mortarity risk from
current anorexia nervosa may be rower than formerty supposed and that
it is not confined to young adutts and adolescents.
That's armost exactry the same number of peopre who annuarly die in
schoor-transportation rerated crashes, which amounts lo L42 annuar
deaths. Considering that far more people eat than go to school, it
should be obvious that unless one considers school transportation to be

stuff that in your piehore, fatty. Better yet, stick your finger down

Slender \iromen are not only healthier than fat \^7omen, t,hey are

18 myserf. Your mireage may vary, but anything over 21 is getting a bit

Everything can be taken to a dangerous extreme, even drinking water.
But that doesn't mean that one should conclude that drinking water is a
deadly danger best avoided. The pejorative terms "eating disorder,' and
"anorexia" should be reserved for that tiny percentage of men and women
who are actually at serious risk of starving themselves to death. For
the overwherming majority, being srenderexy shourd be considered
something that is desirable, fortunate, and beneficiar for women.

Surveys -- one of the leading deaüs of businesses (well,I woutdl.lbe surprised if
many a businesses first death *orr.rá blows was from a survey, at least)' Why? Because
people are notorious about telling you what they say they want (or what you want to hear)
and not what they actually want. Go back to the Patrice'O'Neal snrff at the beginning of
this example. Survey 10 rándom hot, sexy women and ask what they want in a man' I
would bet green *or.y they would say things like 'A man who is sweet, and kind and
shares his feetings , etc'; But, then tail each oi those women back home (wait' that sounds
bad...) and look at who they actually pine for ald go with:Jerks, assholes and douchebags'
It's a perfect example of this pheno..retton and a smart email marketer wanting to use
,rr*.y, in a way that doesn't tlo* up in thy face would do well to think of your customers
like hot, sexy women saying they love a nice guy, but actually get turned on by üe

l. Ask thern what they just bought. (The more recent, the
better) As well as what the URL s are, etc. This lets )¡ou go see what
they are actuall)¡ spending mone), on now -- including the appeals,

2.To gather testirnonials. In fact, here is a HUGE tip for your
business if you want start raking in piles of testimonials and lots of
"fodder" for your emails: If )rou are going to do a survex ask lor
them to tell you a story about )¡our product. And you do this by
simply asking them to specifically tell you a story about what they
liked about the product, how they came across it, what problem they
were looking to solve, what the results were, etc. (Obüously, you are
sending this kind of survey to buyers).
This will give you not only a bunch of testimonials you can use (real, useful
testimonials, not "golly, I sure liked the color of the package!!!" testimonials). But
testimonials with some fraggin teeth. Testimonials you can, of course, work into your
marketing (like emails, etc). But, besides that, those testimonials then become the basis of
entire email campaigns, too.
Think about it:
There are the words your market is using. There are the problems they had that
your product is solving. There are the pains and desires that plague them, and how your
product helped them get out of whatever terrible ordeals they were in (or get the desires
they were looking for). I have used this to write dozens of emails for products for multiple
markets. I remember writing a series of 100 emails for an auto-responder in the biz opp
market a few years back. And, I structured it so every 5th email was a testimonial story
based email. In their case they had lots of testimonials already. But now, by using this
technique as I've described, you can gain lots of "story" based testimonials for your email
broadcasts, auto-responder sequences, sales letters and anything else, too.
I hope you understand just how powerful this one tip is.
Use it, love it and, yes, profit from it.
